---
title: 'Modelling of Tau, pTau and beta-amyloid biomarkers with polyol-based signature'
author: 
    - name: 'Marc Clos-Garcia'
      affiliation: 'Steno Diabetes Center Copenhagen, Denmark'
      email: 'marc.clos.garcia@regiong.dk'
date: last-modified
date-format: "D MMMM, YYYY"
format: 
    html:
        toc: true
        toc-depth: 3
        number-sections: true
        code-fold: true
        code-summary: 'Click to expand/collapse code'
        theme: cosmo
---


```{python import libraries}
# Import necessary libraries
import os as os
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.linear_model import ElasticNetCV
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.model_selection import permutation_test_score, KFold
from sklearn.model_selection import cross_val_predict # for MetabolitRiskScore computation for mediation analysis (later)
from sklearn.metrics import r2_score, mean_squared_error, mean_absolute_error
import shap
import pickle
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

from IPython.display import display
from ipywidgets import FloatProgress

# Set random seed for reproducibility
np.random.seed(42)

```


In this notebook, we have developed an Elastic Net regression modelling approach to predict CSF biomarkers (tTau, pTau and beta-amyloid) from a polyol-based metabolite signature, with and without confounding variables (age, gender, BMI and statins). We have also performed permutation testing to assess the statistical significance of the models.

```{python}
os.getcwd()
models_dir = '../results/modelling/'
```


```{python}
## define functions needed later
def plot_pred_vs_actual(ax, y, y_pred, r2, score, pvalue, rmse, mae, title):
    # Scatter plot of actual vs predicted
    ax.scatter(y, y_pred, alpha=0.6, s=50, edgecolors='black', linewidth=0.5)
    # Perfect prediction line
    min_val = min(y.min(), y_pred.min())
    max_val = max(y.max(), y_pred.max())
    ax.plot([min_val, max_val], [min_val, max_val], 'r--', linewidth=2, label='Perfect prediction')
    # Labels and title
    ax.set_xlabel('Actual', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
    ax.set_ylabel('Predicted', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
    ax.set_title(title, fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
    # Stats box
    stats_text = f'N = {len(y)}\nRMSE = {rmse:.2f}\nMAE = {mae:.2f}'
    ax.text(0.05, 0.95, stats_text, transform=ax.transAxes, 
            verticalalignment='top', bbox=dict(boxstyle='round', facecolor='wheat', alpha=0.5),
            fontsize=10)
    ax.legend(fontsize=9)
    ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
```

```{python}
# Load & inspect data
# df = pd.read_csv('../data/csf_ratios.csv', sep=';', encoding='latin1')
df = pd.read_csv('../data/csf_ratios2.csv', sep=';', encoding='latin1')

print(f'\nOriginal dataset shape: {df.shape}')
print(f'\nColumn names:\n{df.columns.tolist()}')
print(df.head())
```


## Evaluate data missingness

Considering we want to predict tTau, pTau and beta-amyloid scores from metabolites abundance data, with and without confounding features, we will need first to evaluate the presence of missing values in the data.

```{python}
# Missing data
missing_summary = pd.DataFrame({
    'Missing_Count': df.isnull().sum(),
    'Missing_Percentage': (df.isnull().sum() / len(df) * 100).round(2)
})
missing_summary = missing_summary[missing_summary['Missing_Count'] > 0]
print(missing_summary)
```

Neither age nor gender have any missing value, while BMI and statins have a low to moderate amount of missing data, so we can evaluate their inclusion on the analysis. CSF glucose levels measurements have an unacceptable levels of missigness, so it won't be considered.


## Targets data summary

Below, we show the targets data summary statistics. Both tau protein (tTau and pTau) measurements have been log-transformed.

```{python}
df['TAU'] = StandardScaler().fit_transform(np.log(df['TAU']).values.reshape(-1,1)).ravel() 
df['PTAU'] = StandardScaler().fit_transform(np.log(df['PTAU']).values.reshape(-1,1)).ravel() 
df['ABETA42'] = StandardScaler().fit_transform(df['ABETA42'].values.reshape(-1,1)).ravel()

print(f'\n\nTarget variable (tTau) statistics:')
print(df['TAU'].describe())
print(f'Missing tTau values: {df['TAU'].isnull().sum()}')

print(f'\n\nTarget variable (pTau) statistics:')
print(df['PTAU'].describe())
print(f'Missing pTau values: {df['PTAU'].isnull().sum()}')

print(f'\n\nTarget variable (AÎ²42) statistics:')
print(df['ABETA42'].describe())
print(f'Missing AÎ²42 values: {df['ABETA42'].isnull().sum()}')
```


## Models

:::{.panel-tabset}

## Models without any confounders

```{python}
# Define the 15 metabolites and permitted confounders
metabolites = [
    'd.Glucose', 'gluc.erythitol', 'd.Galactose', 'gluc.sorbitol', 
    'D.....Xylose', 'D.....Ribofuranose', 'Sorbitol', 'L.....Arabitol', 
    'D.Threitol', 'meso.Erythritol', 'X3.Deoxy.erythro.pentitol', 
    'X1.5.Anhydrohexitol', 'Ribonic.acid', 'L.Threonic.acid', 'Glyceric.acid'
]

confounders = []

print(f'\n15 Metabolites: {metabolites}')
print(f'\n0 Confounders: {confounders}')

# Check metabolite completeness
for metabolite in metabolites:
    missing = df[metabolite].isnull().sum()
    print(f'  {metabolite}: {missing} missing values')
```

### tTau modelling

```{python}
# Create a working dataframe with only the variables we need
working_cols = metabolites + confounders + ['TAU']
df_working = df[working_cols].copy()

print(f'Working dataset shape before complete-case: {df_working.shape}')
print(df_working.isnull().sum()[df_working.isnull().sum() > 0])
# Verify no missing data
print(f'\nMissing values in complete dataset: {df_working.isnull().sum().sum()}')
```

Model training parameters:
```{python}
## import results from python run
res_path = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), models_dir, 'results_metabolites_only_TAU.pkl'))
with open(res_path, 'rb') as f:
    res_tau = pickle.load(f)


# --- Optimal parameters ---
print(f'  - Alpha: {res_tau["alpha"]:.6f}')
print(f'  - L1 ratio: {res_tau["l1_ratio"]:.6f}')

```

Model metrics:
```{python}
# --- Metrics ---
print(f'\nRÂ² Score: {res_tau["r2"]:.4f}')
print(f'RMSE: {res_tau["rmse"]:.2f}')
print(f'MAE: {res_tau["mae"]:.2f}')

print(f'\nTarget (tTau) mean: {df_working['TAU'].mean():.2f}')
print(f'Target (tTau) std: {df_working['TAU'].std():.2f}')
```

Coefficients table:
```{python}
# Create a dataframe of coefficients
coef_df_ttau = pd.DataFrame({
    'Feature': res_tau['feature_columns'],
    'Coefficient': res_tau['coefficients'],
    'Abs_Coefficient': np.abs(res_tau['coefficients'])
})

# Sort by absolute value
coef_df = coef_df_ttau.sort_values('Abs_Coefficient', ascending=False)
print(coef_df.to_string(index=False))
```

10,000 permutation scores_tau:
```{python}
print(f'\nTrue RÂ² score (from CV): {res_tau['true_r2_cv']:.4f}')
print(f'Mean RÂ² from permutations: {res_tau['permutation_scores'].mean():.4f}')
print(f'Std RÂ² from permutations: {res_tau['permutation_scores'].std():.4f}')
print(f'P-value: {res_tau['p_value']:.4f}')
print(f"  - The model's RÂ² of {res_tau['true_r2_cv']:.4f} is being compared to {len(res_tau['permutation_scores'])} models")
print(f'    trained on randomly permuted target values.')
print(f'  - A p-value of {res_tau['p_value']:.4f} indicates that {res_tau['p_value']*100:.2f}% of permuted models')
print(f'    performed as well or better than the true model.')
```

#### Permutation test plot
```{python}
# permutation plot
afig, ax = plt.subplots(1, 1, figsize=(10, 6))

ax.axvline(x=res_tau['permutation_scores'].mean(), color='gray', linestyle=':', linewidth=2, 
           label=f'Mean permuted RÂ² = {res_tau['permutation_scores'].mean():.4f}')

# Plot histogram of permutation scores_tau
ax.hist(res_tau['permutation_scores'], bins=50, color='lightblue', edgecolor='black', alpha=0.7)

# Add vertical line for true score
ax.axvline(x=res_tau['true_r2_cv'], color='red', linestyle='--', linewidth=2, label=f'True RÂ² = {res_tau['true_r2_cv']:.4f}')

# Add vertical line for mean of permutations
ax.set_xlabel('RÂ² Score', fontsize=12)
ax.set_ylabel('Frequency', fontsize=12)
ax.set_title(f'Permutation Test for tTau Prediction Model\n(p = {res_tau['p_value']:.4f}, n_permutations = 1000)', 
             fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
ax.legend(fontsize=10)
ax.grid(axis='y', alpha=0.3)

plt.tight_layout()
res_tau_path = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), models_dir, 'permutation_test_TAU_onlymets.png'))
plt.savefig(res_tau_path, dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')
print('\nPermutation test histogram saved as "permutation_test_TAU_onlymets.png"')
plt.show()
```

#### tTau predicted vs actual values plot
```{python}
# Create a final visualization showing predicted vs actual TAU values
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 8))
plot_pred_vs_actual(ax, res_tau['y_true'], res_tau['y_pred'], res_tau['r2'], res_tau['true_r2_cv'], res_tau['p_value'], res_tau['rmse'], res_tau['mae'], 'Elastic Net Model: Predicted vs Actual tTau\n' + 
             f'RÂ² (full fit) = {res_tau['r2']:.4f}, RÂ² (CV) = {res_tau['true_r2_cv']:.4f}, p = {res_tau['p_value']:.4f}')
plt.tight_layout()
res_tau_path = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), models_dir, 'TAU_onlymets_predictionresults.png'))
plt.savefig(res_tau_path, dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')
print('\nPrediction scatter plot saved as "TAU_onlymets_predictionresults.png"')
plt.show()
```


### pTau modelling

```{python}
# Create a working dataframe with only the variables we need
working_cols = metabolites + confounders + ['PTAU']
df_working = df[working_cols].copy()

print(f'Working dataset shape before complete-case: {df_working.shape}')
print(df_working.isnull().sum()[df_working.isnull().sum() > 0])
# Verify no missing data
print(f'\nMissing values in complete dataset: {df_working.isnull().sum().sum()}')
```

Model training parameters:
```{python}
## import res_ptauults from python run
res_path = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), models_dir, 'results_metabolites_only_PTAU.pkl'))
with open(res_path, 'rb') as f:
    res_ptau = pickle.load(f)


# --- Optimal parameters ---
print(f'  - Alpha: {res_ptau["alpha"]:.6f}')
print(f'  - L1 ratio: {res_ptau["l1_ratio"]:.6f}')

```

Model metrics:
```{python}
# --- Metrics ---
print(f'\nRÂ² Score: {res_ptau["r2"]:.4f}')
print(f'RMSE: {res_ptau["rmse"]:.2f}')
print(f'MAE: {res_ptau["mae"]:.2f}')

print(f'\nTarget (pTau) mean: {df_working['PTAU'].mean():.2f}')
print(f'Target (pTau) std: {df_working['PTAU'].std():.2f}')
```

Coefficients table:
```{python}
# Create a dataframe of coefficients
coef_df_ptau = pd.DataFrame({
    'Feature': res_ptau['feature_columns'],
    'Coefficient': res_ptau['coefficients'],
    'Abs_Coefficient': np.abs(res_ptau['coefficients'])
})

# Sort by absolute value
coef_df = coef_df_ptau.sort_values('Abs_Coefficient', ascending=False)
print(coef_df.to_string(index=False))
```

10,000 permutation scores_ptau:
```{python}
print(f'\nTrue RÂ² score (from CV): {res_ptau['true_r2_cv']:.4f}')
print(f'Mean RÂ² from permutations: {res_ptau['permutation_scores'].mean():.4f}')
print(f'Std RÂ² from permutations: {res_ptau['permutation_scores'].std():.4f}')
print(f'P-value: {res_ptau['p_value']:.4f}')
print(f"  - The model's RÂ² of {res_ptau['true_r2_cv']:.4f} is being compared to {len(res_ptau['permutation_scores'])} models")
print(f'    trained on randomly permuted target values.')
print(f'  - A p-value of {res_ptau['p_value']:.4f} indicates that {res_ptau['p_value']*100:.2f}% of permuted models')
print(f'    performed as well or better than the true model.')
```

#### Permutation test plot

```{python}
# permutation plot
afig, ax = plt.subplots(1, 1, figsize=(10, 6))

ax.axvline(x=res_ptau['permutation_scores'].mean(), color='gray', linestyle=':', linewidth=2, 
           label=f'Mean permuted RÂ² = {res_ptau['permutation_scores'].mean():.4f}')

# Plot histogram of permutation scores_ptau
ax.hist(res_ptau['permutation_scores'], bins=50, color='lightblue', edgecolor='black', alpha=0.7)

# Add vertical line for true score
ax.axvline(x=res_ptau['true_r2_cv'], color='red', linestyle='--', linewidth=2, label=f'True RÂ² = {res_ptau['true_r2_cv']:.4f}')

# Add vertical line for mean of permutations
ax.set_xlabel('RÂ² Score', fontsize=12)
ax.set_ylabel('Frequency', fontsize=12)
ax.set_title(f'Permutation Test for pTau Prediction Model\n(p = {res_ptau['p_value']:.4f}, n_permutations = 1000)', 
             fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
ax.legend(fontsize=10)
ax.grid(axis='y', alpha=0.3)

plt.tight_layout()
res_ptau_path = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), models_dir, 'permutation_test_PTAU_onlymets.png'))
plt.savefig(res_ptau_path, dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')
print('\nPermutation test histogram saved as "permutation_test_PTAU_onlymets.png"')
plt.show()
```

#### pTau predicted vs actual values plot
```{python}
# Create a final visualization showing predicted vs actual TAU values
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 8))
plot_pred_vs_actual(ax, res_ptau['y_true'], res_ptau['y_pred'], res_ptau['r2'], res_ptau['true_r2_cv'], res_ptau['p_value'], res_ptau['rmse'], res_ptau['mae'], 'Elastic Net Model: Predicted vs Actual pTau\n' + 
             f'RÂ² (full fit) = {res_ptau['r2']:.4f}, RÂ² (CV) = {res_ptau['true_r2_cv']:.4f}, p = {res_ptau['p_value']:.4f}')
plt.tight_layout()
res_ptau_path = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), models_dir, 'PTAU_onlymets_predictionresults.png'))
plt.savefig(res_ptau_path, dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')
print('\nPrediction scatter plot saved as "PTAU_onlymets_predictionresults.png"')
plt.show()
```

### AÎ²42 modelling

```{python}
# Create a working dataframe with only the variables we need
working_cols = metabolites + confounders + ['ABETA42']
df_working = df[working_cols].copy()

print(f'Working dataset shape before complete-case: {df_working.shape}')
print(df_working.isnull().sum()[df_working.isnull().sum() > 0])
# Verify no missing data
print(f'\nMissing values in complete dataset: {df_working.isnull().sum().sum()}')
```

Model training parameters:
```{python}
## import results from python run
res_path = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), models_dir, 'results_metabolites_only_ABETA42.pkl'))
with open(res_path, 'rb') as f:
    res_ab42 = pickle.load(f)


# --- Optimal parameters ---
print(f'  - Alpha: {res_ab42["alpha"]:.6f}')
print(f'  - L1 ratio: {res_ab42["l1_ratio"]:.6f}')

```

Model metrics:
```{python}
# --- Metrics ---
print(f'\nRÂ² Score: {res_ab42["r2"]:.4f}')
print(f'RMSE: {res_ab42["rmse"]:.2f}')
print(f'MAE: {res_ab42["mae"]:.2f}')

print(f'\nTarget (AÎ²42) mean: {df_working['ABETA42'].mean():.2f}')
print(f'Target (AÎ²42) std: {df_working['ABETA42'].std():.2f}')
```

Coefficients table:
```{python}
# Create a dataframe of coefficients
coef_df_ab42 = pd.DataFrame({
    'Feature': res_ab42['feature_columns'],
    'Coefficient': res_ab42['coefficients'],
    'Abs_Coefficient': np.abs(res_ab42['coefficients'])
})

# Sort by absolute value
coef_df = coef_df_ab42.sort_values('Abs_Coefficient', ascending=False)
print(coef_df.to_string(index=False))
```

10,000 permutation scores_ab42:
```{python}
print(f'\nTrue RÂ² score (from CV): {res_ab42['true_r2_cv']:.4f}')
print(f'Mean RÂ² from permutations: {res_ab42['permutation_scores'].mean():.4f}')
print(f'Std RÂ² from permutations: {res_ab42['permutation_scores'].std():.4f}')
print(f'P-value: {res_ab42['p_value']:.4f}')
print(f"  - The model's RÂ² of {res_ab42['true_r2_cv']:.4f} is being compared to {len(res_ab42['permutation_scores'])} models")
print(f'    trained on randomly permuted target values.')
print(f'  - A p-value of {res_ab42['p_value']:.4f} indicates that {res_ab42['p_value']*100:.2f}% of permuted models')
print(f'    performed as well or better than the true model.')
```

#### Permutation test plot

```{python}
# permutation plot
afig, ax = plt.subplots(1, 1, figsize=(10, 6))

ax.axvline(x=res_ab42['permutation_scores'].mean(), color='gray', linestyle=':', linewidth=2, 
           label=f'Mean permuted RÂ² = {res_ab42['permutation_scores'].mean():.4f}')

# Plot histogram of permutation scores_ab42
ax.hist(res_ab42['permutation_scores'], bins=50, color='lightblue', edgecolor='black', alpha=0.7)

# Add vertical line for true score
ax.axvline(x=res_ab42['true_r2_cv'], color='red', linestyle='--', linewidth=2, label=f'True RÂ² = {res_ab42['true_r2_cv']:.4f}')

# Add vertical line for mean of permutations
ax.set_xlabel('RÂ² Score', fontsize=12)
ax.set_ylabel('Frequency', fontsize=12)
ax.set_title(f'Permutation Test for AÎ²42 Prediction Model\n(p = {res_ab42['p_value']:.4f}, n_permutations = 1000)', 
             fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
ax.legend(fontsize=10)
ax.grid(axis='y', alpha=0.3)

plt.tight_layout()
res_ab42_path = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), models_dir, 'permutation_test_ABETA42_onlymets.png'))
plt.savefig(res_ab42_path, dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')
print('\nPermutation test histogram saved as "permutation_test_ABETA42_onlymets.png"')
plt.show()
```

#### AÎ²42 predicted vs actual values plot
```{python}
# Create a final visualization showing predicted vs actual TAU values
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 8))
plot_pred_vs_actual(ax, res_ab42['y_true'], res_ab42['y_pred'], res_ab42['r2'], res_ab42['true_r2_cv'], res_ab42['p_value'], res_ab42['rmse'], res_ab42['mae'], 'Elastic Net Model: Predicted vs Actual AÎ²42\n' + 
             f'RÂ² (full fit) = {res_ab42['r2']:.4f}, RÂ² (CV) = {res_ab42['true_r2_cv']:.4f}, p = {res_ab42['p_value']:.4f}')
plt.tight_layout()
res_ab42_path = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), models_dir, 'ABETA42_onlymets_predictionresults.png'))
plt.savefig(res_ab42_path, dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')
print('\nPrediction scatter plot saved as "ABETA42_onlymets_predictionresults.png"')
plt.show()
```


### Predicted vs actual plots - combined biomarkers
```{python}
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(20, 6))
plot_pred_vs_actual(axes[0],  res_tau['y_true'], res_tau['y_pred'], res_tau['r2'], res_tau['true_r2_cv'], 
res_tau['p_value'], res_tau['rmse'], res_tau['mae'], 'tTau\n' + 
             f'RÂ² (full fit) = {res_tau['r2']:.4f}, RÂ² (CV) = {res_tau['true_r2_cv']:.4f}, p = {res_tau['p_value']:.4f}')
plot_pred_vs_actual(axes[1],  res_ptau['y_true'], res_ptau['y_pred'], res_ptau['r2'], res_ptau['true_r2_cv'], res_ptau['p_value'], res_ptau['rmse'], res_ptau['mae'], 'pTau\n' + 
             f'RÂ² (full fit) = {res_ptau['r2']:.4f}, RÂ² (CV) = {res_ptau['true_r2_cv']:.4f}, p = {res_ptau['p_value']:.4f}')
plot_pred_vs_actual(axes[2], res_ab42['y_true'], res_ab42['y_pred'], res_ab42['r2'], res_ab42['true_r2_cv'], res_ab42['p_value'], res_ab42['rmse'], res_ab42['mae'], 'AÎ²42\n' + 
             f'RÂ² (full fit) = {res_ab42['r2']:.4f}, RÂ² (CV) = {res_ab42['true_r2_cv']:.4f}, p = {res_ab42['p_value']:.4f}')

plt.tight_layout()
res_path = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), models_dir, 'ALL_onlymets_prediction_results.png'))
plt.savefig(res_path, dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')
print('\Real vs predicted models saved as "ALL_onlymets_prediction_results.png"')
plt.show()
```

Final coefficients table for all biomarkers:
```{python}
# create one single coefficients dataframe
## rename columns so it dont have the same names
coef_df_ttau = coef_df_ttau.set_index('Feature').rename(
    columns=lambda c: f'{c}_tTau'
)

coef_df_ptau = coef_df_ptau.set_index('Feature').rename(
    columns=lambda c: f'{c}_pTau'
)

coef_df_ab42 = coef_df_ab42.set_index('Feature').rename(
    columns=lambda c: f'{c}_AB42'
)

## join them all
coefficients_all_df = coef_df_ttau.join([coef_df_ptau, coef_df_ab42])

res_path = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), models_dir, 'ALL_onlymets_coefficients_df.txt'))
coefficients_all_df.round(3).to_csv(res_path, sep='\t')
print('Coefficients dataframe saved as "ALL_onlymets_coefficients_df.txt"')
```

```{python}
display(
    coefficients_all_df
        .round(3)
        .sort_values('Abs_Coefficient_tTau', ascending=False)
)
```



## Models with confounders - Age and Gender (no missing values)
```{python}
# Define the 15 metabolites and permitted confounders
metabolites = [
    'd.Glucose', 'gluc.erythitol', 'd.Galactose', 'gluc.sorbitol', 
    'D.....Xylose', 'D.....Ribofuranose', 'Sorbitol', 'L.....Arabitol', 
    'D.Threitol', 'meso.Erythritol', 'X3.Deoxy.erythro.pentitol', 
    'X1.5.Anhydrohexitol', 'Ribonic.acid', 'L.Threonic.acid', 'Glyceric.acid'
]

confounders = ['AgeAtVisit', 'Gender']

print(f'\n15 Metabolites: {metabolites}')
print(f'\n2 Confounders: {confounders}')

# Check metabolite completeness
for metabolite in metabolites:
    missing = df[metabolite].isnull().sum()
    print(f'  {metabolite}: {missing} missing values')
```

### tTau modelling

```{python}
# Create a working dataframe with only the variables we need
working_cols = metabolites + confounders + ['TAU']
df_working = df[working_cols].copy()

print(f'Working dataset shape before complete-case: {df_working.shape}')
print(df_working.isnull().sum()[df_working.isnull().sum() > 0])
# Verify no missing data
print(f'\nMissing values in complete dataset: {df_working.isnull().sum().sum()}')
```

Model training parameters:
```{python}
## import results from python run
res_path = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), models_dir, 'results_metabolites_plus_age_gender_TAU.pkl'))
with open(res_path, 'rb') as f:
    res_tau = pickle.load(f)


# --- Optimal parameters ---
print(f'  - Alpha: {res_tau["alpha"]:.6f}')
print(f'  - L1 ratio: {res_tau["l1_ratio"]:.6f}')

```

Model metrics:
```{python}
# --- Metrics ---
print(f'\nRÂ² Score: {res_tau["r2"]:.4f}')
print(f'RMSE: {res_tau["rmse"]:.2f}')
print(f'MAE: {res_tau["mae"]:.2f}')

print(f'\nTarget (tTau) mean: {df_working['TAU'].mean():.2f}')
print(f'Target (tTau) std: {df_working['TAU'].std():.2f}')
```

Coefficients table:
```{python}
# Create a dataframe of coefficients
coef_df_ttau = pd.DataFrame({
    'Feature': res_tau['feature_columns'],
    'Coefficient': res_tau['coefficients'],
    'Abs_Coefficient': np.abs(res_tau['coefficients'])
})

# Sort by absolute value
coef_df = coef_df_ttau.sort_values('Abs_Coefficient', ascending=False)
print(coef_df.to_string(index=False))

# create one single coefficients dataframe
## rename columns so it dont have the same names
coef_df_ttau = coef_df_ttau.set_index('Feature').rename(
    columns=lambda c: f'{c}_tTau_AgeGender'
)
```

10,000 permutation scores_tau:
```{python}
print(f'\nTrue RÂ² score (from CV): {res_tau['true_r2_cv']:.4f}')
print(f'Mean RÂ² from permutations: {res_tau['permutation_scores'].mean():.4f}')
print(f'Std RÂ² from permutations: {res_tau['permutation_scores'].std():.4f}')
print(f'P-value: {res_tau['p_value']:.4f}')
print(f"  - The model's RÂ² of {res_tau['true_r2_cv']:.4f} is being compared to {len(res_tau['permutation_scores'])} models")
print(f'    trained on randomly permuted target values.')
print(f'  - A p-value of {res_tau['p_value']:.4f} indicates that {res_tau['p_value']*100:.2f}% of permuted models')
print(f'    performed as well or better than the true model.')
```

#### Permutation test plot

```{python}
# permutation plot
afig, ax = plt.subplots(1, 1, figsize=(10, 6))

ax.axvline(x=res_tau['permutation_scores'].mean(), color='gray', linestyle=':', linewidth=2, 
           label=f'Mean permuted RÂ² = {res_tau['permutation_scores'].mean():.4f}')

# Plot histogram of permutation scores_tau
ax.hist(res_tau['permutation_scores'], bins=50, color='lightblue', edgecolor='black', alpha=0.7)

# Add vertical line for true score
ax.axvline(x=res_tau['true_r2_cv'], color='red', linestyle='--', linewidth=2, label=f'True RÂ² = {res_tau['true_r2_cv']:.4f}')

# Add vertical line for mean of permutations
ax.set_xlabel('RÂ² Score', fontsize=12)
ax.set_ylabel('Frequency', fontsize=12)
ax.set_title(f'Permutation Test for tTau (+ Age + Gender) Prediction Model\n(p = {res_tau['p_value']:.4f}, n_permutations = 1000)', 
             fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
ax.legend(fontsize=10)
ax.grid(axis='y', alpha=0.3)

plt.tight_layout()
res_tau_path = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), models_dir, 'permutation_test_TAU_AgeGender.png'))
plt.savefig(res_tau_path, dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')
print('\nPermutation test histogram saved as "permutation_test_TAU_AgeGender.png"')
plt.show()
```

#### tTau predicted vs actual values plot
```{python}
# Create a final visualization showing predicted vs actual TAU values
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 8))
plot_pred_vs_actual(ax, res_tau['y_true'], res_tau['y_pred'], res_tau['r2'], res_tau['true_r2_cv'], res_tau['p_value'], res_tau['rmse'], res_tau['mae'], 'Elastic Net Model: Predicted vs Actual tTau (+ Age + Gender)\n' + 
             f'RÂ² (full fit) = {res_tau['r2']:.4f}, RÂ² (CV) = {res_tau['true_r2_cv']:.4f}, p = {res_tau['p_value']:.4f}')
plt.tight_layout()
res_tau_path = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), models_dir, 'TAU_AgeGender_predictionresults.png'))
plt.savefig(res_tau_path, dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')
print('\nPrediction scatter plot saved as "TAU_AgeGender_predictionresults.png"')
plt.show()
```


#### SHAP values plot and feature importance
```{python}
# extract objects for SHAP plot
model = res_tau['fitted_model']
X_scaled = res_tau['X_scaled']

# extract the elastic net model from the pipeline
elastic_net_model = model.named_steps['enet']

# compute SHAP values
explainer = shap.LinearExplainer(
    elastic_net_model,
    X_scaled,
    feature_columns= res_tau['feature_columns']
)
shap_values = explainer.shap_values(X_scaled)

# Create a dictionary to map feature names to nice labels for publishing
feature_labels = {
    'd.Glucose': 'd-Glucose',
    'gluc.erythitol': 'd-Glucose / Erythritol',
    'd.Galactose': 'd-Galactose',
    'gluc.sorbitol': 'd-Glucose / Sorbitol',
    'D.....Xylose': 'D-(-)-Xylose',
    'D.....Ribofuranose': 'D-Ribofuranose',
    'Sorbitol': 'Sorbitol',
    'L.....Arabitol': 'L-(-)-Arabitol',
    'D.Threitol': 'D-Threitol',
    'meso.Erythritol': 'meso-Erythritol',
    'X3.Deoxy.erythro.pentitol': '3-Deoxy-erythro-pentitol',
    'X1.5.Anhydrohexitol': '1,5-Anhydrohexitol',
    'Ribonic.acid': 'Ribonic acid',
    'L.Threonic.acid': 'L-Threonic acid',
    'Glyceric.acid': 'Glyceric acid'
}

# Map feature names to labels
nice_feature_names = [feature_labels.get(col, col) for col in res_tau['feature_columns']]

shap.summary_plot(shap_values, X_scaled, feature_names=nice_feature_names)

```

### pTau modelling

```{python}
# Create a working dataframe with only the variables we need
working_cols = metabolites + confounders + ['PTAU']
df_working = df[working_cols].copy()

print(f'Working dataset shape before complete-case: {df_working.shape}')
print(df_working.isnull().sum()[df_working.isnull().sum() > 0])
# Verify no missing data
print(f'\nMissing values in complete dataset: {df_working.isnull().sum().sum()}')
```

Model training parameters:
```{python}
## import res_ptauults from python run
res_path = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), models_dir, 'results_metabolites_plus_age_gender_PTAU.pkl'))
with open(res_path, 'rb') as f:
    res_ptau = pickle.load(f)


# --- Optimal parameters ---
print(f'  - Alpha: {res_ptau["alpha"]:.6f}')
print(f'  - L1 ratio: {res_ptau["l1_ratio"]:.6f}')

```

Model metrics:
```{python}
# --- Metrics ---
print(f'\nRÂ² Score: {res_ptau["r2"]:.4f}')
print(f'RMSE: {res_ptau["rmse"]:.2f}')
print(f'MAE: {res_ptau["mae"]:.2f}')

print(f'\nTarget (pTau) mean: {df_working['PTAU'].mean():.2f}')
print(f'Target (pTau) std: {df_working['PTAU'].std():.2f}')
```

Coefficients table:
```{python}
# Create a dataframe of coefficients
coef_df_ptau = pd.DataFrame({
    'Feature': res_ptau['feature_columns'],
    'Coefficient': res_ptau['coefficients'],
    'Abs_Coefficient': np.abs(res_ptau['coefficients'])
})

# Sort by absolute value
coef_df = coef_df_ptau.sort_values('Abs_Coefficient', ascending=False)
print(coef_df.to_string(index=False))

# create one single coefficients dataframe
## rename columns so it dont have the same names
coef_df_ptau = coef_df_ptau.set_index('Feature').rename(
    columns=lambda c: f'{c}_pTau_AgeGender'
)
```

10,000 permutation scores_ptau:
```{python}
print(f'\nTrue RÂ² score (from CV): {res_ptau['true_r2_cv']:.4f}')
print(f'Mean RÂ² from permutations: {res_ptau['permutation_scores'].mean():.4f}')
print(f'Std RÂ² from permutations: {res_ptau['permutation_scores'].std():.4f}')
print(f'P-value: {res_ptau['p_value']:.4f}')
print(f"  - The model's RÂ² of {res_ptau['true_r2_cv']:.4f} is being compared to {len(res_ptau['permutation_scores'])} models")
print(f'    trained on randomly permuted target values.')
print(f'  - A p-value of {res_ptau['p_value']:.4f} indicates that {res_ptau['p_value']*100:.2f}% of permuted models')
print(f'    performed as well or better than the true model.')
```

#### Permutation test plot

```{python}
# permutation plot
afig, ax = plt.subplots(1, 1, figsize=(10, 6))

ax.axvline(x=res_ptau['permutation_scores'].mean(), color='gray', linestyle=':', linewidth=2, 
           label=f'Mean permuted RÂ² = {res_ptau['permutation_scores'].mean():.4f}')

# Plot histogram of permutation scores_ptau
ax.hist(res_ptau['permutation_scores'], bins=50, color='lightblue', edgecolor='black', alpha=0.7)

# Add vertical line for true score
ax.axvline(x=res_ptau['true_r2_cv'], color='red', linestyle='--', linewidth=2, label=f'True RÂ² = {res_ptau['true_r2_cv']:.4f}')

# Add vertical line for mean of permutations
ax.set_xlabel('RÂ² Score', fontsize=12)
ax.set_ylabel('Frequency', fontsize=12)
ax.set_title(f'Permutation Test for pTau Prediction Model (+ Age + Gender)\n(p = {res_ptau['p_value']:.4f}, n_permutations = 1000)', 
             fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
ax.legend(fontsize=10)
ax.grid(axis='y', alpha=0.3)

plt.tight_layout()
res_ptau_path = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), models_dir, 'permutation_test_PTAU_AgeGender.png'))
plt.savefig(res_ptau_path, dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')
print('\nPermutation test histogram saved as "permutation_test_PTAU_AgeGender.png"')
plt.show()
```

#### pTau predicted vs actual values plot
```{python}
# Create a final visualization showing predicted vs actual TAU values
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 8))
plot_pred_vs_actual(ax, res_ptau['y_true'], res_ptau['y_pred'], res_ptau['r2'], res_ptau['true_r2_cv'], res_ptau['p_value'], res_ptau['rmse'], res_ptau['mae'], 'Elastic Net Model: Predicted vs Actual pTau (+ Age + Gender)\n' + 
             f'RÂ² (full fit) = {res_ptau['r2']:.4f}, RÂ² (CV) = {res_ptau['true_r2_cv']:.4f}, p = {res_ptau['p_value']:.4f}')
plt.tight_layout()
res_ptau_path = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), models_dir, 'PTAU_AgeGender_predictionresults.png'))
plt.savefig(res_ptau_path, dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')
print('\nPrediction scatter plot saved as "PTAU_AgeGender_predictionresults.png"')
plt.show()
```



#### SHAP values plot and feature importance
```{python}
# extract objects for SHAP plot
model = res_ptau['fitted_model']
X_scaled = res_ptau['X_scaled']

# extract the elastic net model from the pipeline
elastic_net_model = model.named_steps['enet']

# compute SHAP values
explainer = shap.LinearExplainer(
    elastic_net_model,
    X_scaled,
    feature_columns= res_tau['feature_columns']
)
shap_values = explainer.shap_values(X_scaled)

# Map feature names to labels
nice_feature_names = [feature_labels.get(col, col) for col in res_ptau['feature_columns']]

shap.summary_plot(shap_values, X_scaled, feature_names=nice_feature_names)

```

### AÎ²42 modelling

```{python}
# Create a working dataframe with only the variables we need
working_cols = metabolites + confounders + ['ABETA42']
df_working = df[working_cols].copy()

print(f'Working dataset shape before complete-case: {df_working.shape}')
print(df_working.isnull().sum()[df_working.isnull().sum() > 0])
# Verify no missing data
print(f'\nMissing values in complete dataset: {df_working.isnull().sum().sum()}')
```

Model training parameters:
```{python}
## import results from python run
res_path = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), models_dir, 'results_metabolites_plus_age_gender_ABETA42.pkl'))
with open(res_path, 'rb') as f:
    res_ab42 = pickle.load(f)


# --- Optimal parameters ---
print(f'  - Alpha: {res_ab42["alpha"]:.6f}')
print(f'  - L1 ratio: {res_ab42["l1_ratio"]:.6f}')

```

Model metrics:
```{python}
# --- Metrics ---
print(f'\nRÂ² Score: {res_ab42["r2"]:.4f}')
print(f'RMSE: {res_ab42["rmse"]:.2f}')
print(f'MAE: {res_ab42["mae"]:.2f}')

print(f'\nTarget (AÎ²42) mean: {df_working['ABETA42'].mean():.2f}')
print(f'Target (AÎ²42) std: {df_working['ABETA42'].std():.2f}')
```

Coefficients table:
```{python}
# Create a dataframe of coefficients
coef_df_ab42 = pd.DataFrame({
    'Feature': res_ab42['feature_columns'],
    'Coefficient': res_ab42['coefficients'],
    'Abs_Coefficient': np.abs(res_ab42['coefficients'])
})

# Sort by absolute value
coef_df = coef_df_ab42.sort_values('Abs_Coefficient', ascending=False)
print(coef_df.to_string(index=False))

## rename columns so it dont have the same names
coef_df_ab42 = coef_df_ab42.set_index('Feature').rename(
    columns=lambda c: f'{c}_AB42_AgeGender'
)
```

10,000 permutation scores_ab42:
```{python}
print(f'\nTrue RÂ² score (from CV): {res_ab42['true_r2_cv']:.4f}')
print(f'Mean RÂ² from permutations: {res_ab42['permutation_scores'].mean():.4f}')
print(f'Std RÂ² from permutations: {res_ab42['permutation_scores'].std():.4f}')
print(f'P-value: {res_ab42['p_value']:.4f}')
print(f"  - The model's RÂ² of {res_ab42['true_r2_cv']:.4f} is being compared to {len(res_ab42['permutation_scores'])} models")
print(f'    trained on randomly permuted target values.')
print(f'  - A p-value of {res_ab42['p_value']:.4f} indicates that {res_ab42['p_value']*100:.2f}% of permuted models')
print(f'    performed as well or better than the true model.')
```

#### Permutation test plot

```{python}
# permutation plot
afig, ax = plt.subplots(1, 1, figsize=(10, 6))

ax.axvline(x=res_ab42['permutation_scores'].mean(), color='gray', linestyle=':', linewidth=2, 
           label=f'Mean permuted RÂ² = {res_ab42['permutation_scores'].mean():.4f}')

# Plot histogram of permutation scores_ab42
ax.hist(res_ab42['permutation_scores'], bins=50, color='lightblue', edgecolor='black', alpha=0.7)

# Add vertical line for true score
ax.axvline(x=res_ab42['true_r2_cv'], color='red', linestyle='--', linewidth=2, label=f'True RÂ² = {res_ab42['true_r2_cv']:.4f}')

# Add vertical line for mean of permutations
ax.set_xlabel('RÂ² Score', fontsize=12)
ax.set_ylabel('Frequency', fontsize=12)
ax.set_title(f'Permutation Test for AÎ²42 Prediction Model (+ Age + Gender)\n(p = {res_ab42['p_value']:.4f}, n_permutations = 1000)', 
             fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
ax.legend(fontsize=10)
ax.grid(axis='y', alpha=0.3)

plt.tight_layout()
res_ab42_path = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), models_dir, 'permutation_test_ABETA42_AgeGender.png'))
plt.savefig(res_ab42_path, dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')
print('\nPermutation test histogram saved as "permutation_test_ABETA42_AgeGender.png"')
plt.show()
```

#### AÎ²42 predicted vs actual values plot
```{python}
# Create a final visualization showing predicted vs actual TAU values
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 8))
plot_pred_vs_actual(ax, res_ab42['y_true'], res_ab42['y_pred'], res_ab42['r2'], res_ab42['true_r2_cv'], res_ab42['p_value'], res_ab42['rmse'], res_ab42['mae'], 'Elastic Net Model: Predicted vs Actual AÎ²42 (+ Age + Gender)\n' + 
             f'RÂ² (full fit) = {res_ab42['r2']:.4f}, RÂ² (CV) = {res_ab42['true_r2_cv']:.4f}, p = {res_ab42['p_value']:.4f}')
plt.tight_layout()
res_ab42_path = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), models_dir, 'ABETA42_AgeGender_predictionresults.png'))
plt.savefig(res_ab42_path, dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')
print('\nPrediction scatter plot saved as "ABETA42_AgeGender_predictionresults.png"')
plt.show()
```



#### SHAP values plot and feature importance
```{python}
# extract objects for SHAP plot
model = res_ab42['fitted_model']
X_scaled = res_ab42['X_scaled']

# extract the elastic net model from the pipeline
elastic_net_model = model.named_steps['enet']

# compute SHAP values
explainer = shap.LinearExplainer(
    elastic_net_model,
    X_scaled,
    feature_columns= res_ab42['feature_columns']
)
shap_values = explainer.shap_values(X_scaled)

# Map feature names to labels
nice_feature_names = [feature_labels.get(col, col) for col in res_ab42['feature_columns']]

shap.summary_plot(shap_values, X_scaled, feature_names=nice_feature_names)

```

### Predicted vs actual plots - combined biomarkers
```{python}
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(20, 6))
plot_pred_vs_actual(axes[0],  res_tau['y_true'], res_tau['y_pred'], res_tau['r2'], res_tau['true_r2_cv'], 
res_tau['p_value'], res_tau['rmse'], res_tau['mae'], 'tTau\n' + 
             f'RÂ² (full fit) = {res_tau['r2']:.4f}, RÂ² (CV) = {res_tau['true_r2_cv']:.4f}, p = {res_tau['p_value']:.4f}')
plot_pred_vs_actual(axes[1],  res_ptau['y_true'], res_ptau['y_pred'], res_ptau['r2'], res_ptau['true_r2_cv'], res_ptau['p_value'], res_ptau['rmse'], res_ptau['mae'], 'pTau\n' + 
             f'RÂ² (full fit) = {res_ptau['r2']:.4f}, RÂ² (CV) = {res_ptau['true_r2_cv']:.4f}, p = {res_ptau['p_value']:.4f}')
plot_pred_vs_actual(axes[2], res_ab42['y_true'], res_ab42['y_pred'], res_ab42['r2'], res_ab42['true_r2_cv'], res_ab42['p_value'], res_ab42['rmse'], res_ab42['mae'], 'AÎ²42\n' + 
             f'RÂ² (full fit) = {res_ab42['r2']:.4f}, RÂ² (CV) = {res_ab42['true_r2_cv']:.4f}, p = {res_ab42['p_value']:.4f}')

plt.tight_layout()
res_path = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), models_dir, 'ALL_AgeGender_prediction_results.png'))
plt.savefig(res_path, dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')
print('\Real vs predicted models saved as "ALL_AgeGender_prediction_results.png"')
plt.show()
```

Final coefficients table for all biomarkers:
```{python}
## join them all
coefficients_all_df = coef_df_ttau.join([coef_df_ptau, coef_df_ab42])

res_path = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), models_dir, 'ALL_AgeGender_coefficients_df.txt'))
coefficients_all_df.round(3).to_csv(res_path, sep='\t')
print('Coefficients dataframe saved as "ALL_AgeGender_coefficients_df.txt"')
```

```{python}
display(
    coefficients_all_df
        .round(3)
        .sort_values('Abs_Coefficient_tTau_AgeGender', ascending=False)
)
```




:::
